text: This is a Test.
And So is This.
test1.speaker: speaker1

text: Test of Speaker 2
test2.speaker: speaker2

intro_start.speaker: narrator
text: Father Aldric had a way of delivering terrible news
I remember the afternoon he called me into his study.
He was already seated when I entered, hands folded atop his worn bible.

intro_3.speaker: aldric
text: "Her name is Clara Vos. Fifteen years old. Family lives out past Brennan Road."
"Three days ago, she went down into their cellar and found something,"
"a box, old, no markings the father could describe clearly."
"She opened it. She hasn't been herself since."

intro_7.speaker: speaker1
text: "What do you mean, hasn't been herself?"

intro_8.speaker: aldric
text: "Not sleeping. Not eating. Speaking in a voice that isn't hers."
"Her mother says she woke in the night to find the girl standing in the hallway,"
"perfectly still, reciting something in a language nobody in that house knows."

intro_11.speaker: narrator
text: He slid a manilla folder across the desk. I opened it.
There were notes, a few photographs of the cellar, a short written account from the father.
I didn't read past the first paragraph.

intro_14.speaker: aldric
text: "I would go myself, but this calls for someone who can move quickly if it comes to that."
"And someone the girl might not frighten so easily."
"I am not certain she would not frighten me."

intro_18.speaker: narrator
text: It was the closest I had ever heard him come to admitting something like that.
I closed the folder and told him I understood.
I would be lying if I said I wasn't afraid. But I went anyway.
The next morning I was at the Voss house by nine.
I knocked twice. After a while the door opened to reveal a young woman.

intro_23.speaker: speaker2
text: "You're from the church."

intro_24.speaker: speaker1
text: "Oh... yes, I am. Father Aldric sent me."

intro_26.speaker: speaker1
text: "I assume you're Clara's sister? Are your parents here?"

intro_27.speaker: speaker2
text: "My parents are at the church. I was told to call them when you arrived."
"I haven't yet."

intro_29.speaker: narrator
text: She stepped back and let me in without another word.
She led me down a narrow hallway and stopped midway.
She pointed to a wooden door down the hallway.

intro_32.speaker: speaker2
text: "She's through there. In the back room. She hasn't come out since yesterday morning."

intro_33.speaker: narrator
text: I was wise enough not to ask her why.

intro_34.speaker: speaker1
text: "I'll go in alone."

intro_36.speaker: narrator
text: She looked at me for a moment as if deciding whether to say something else.
Then she nodded and turned to go into another room.
There was a mirror on the wall beside me. I hadn't paid it any attention on the way in.
But standing there alone in the hallway, I found myself turning toward it.
There I am.
I turned away from the mirror and walked to the end of the hallway.
I slowly opened the door.

interstage0_1.speaker: narrator
text: She was sitting on the edge of the bed.

interstage0_2.speaker: clara
text: "Who are you?"

interstage0_3.speaker: speaker1
text: "I'm a priest. A friend of mine asked me to come and see you."

interstage0_4.speaker: clara
text: "Father Aldric?"

interstage0_5.speaker: speaker1
text: "That's right."

interstage0_6.speaker: clara
text: "My mom called him."
"She's been crying a lot."

interstage0_7.speaker: speaker1
text: "I know. That's why I'm here."
"How are you feeling, Clara?"

interstage0_8.speaker: clara
text: "Tired."
"I keep having bad dreams."

interstage0_9.speaker: speaker1
text: "That's okay."
"I'm just going to say a few words, if that's alright with you. It won't take long."

interstage0_10.speaker: clara
text: "Is it going to hurt?"

interstage0_11.speaker: speaker1
text: "No. I promise it won't."

interstage0_12.speaker: clara
text: "Okay."

interstage1_1.speaker: clara
text: "Father?"

interstage1_2.speaker: speaker1
text: "Yes?"

interstage1_3.speaker: clara
text: "Do you know what it felt like? When I opened the box?"

interstage1_4.speaker: speaker1
text: "What did it feel like?"

interstage1_5.speaker: clara
text: "Like something finally coming home."
"That's what it told me, anyway."

interstage1_6.speaker: speaker1
text: "W- what?"

interstage1_7.speaker: clara
text: "You look scared."
"Do you practice in the mirror often?"

interstage1_8.speaker: narrator
text: I took a step back.
My hand tightened around my bible.

interstage1_9.speaker: clara
text: "She's still in here, if that helps."
"It doesn't, does it."

interstage1_11.speaker: clara
text: "Hello, priest."

interstage2_1.speaker: clara
text: "You're doing well."
"Really. Better than the last one."

interstage2_3.speaker: clara
text: "Do you know what happens when you push something out of a vessel?"
"It doesn't disappear."
"There's always somewhere else to be."
"There are so many people, Father."

interstage2_3b.speaker: clara
text: "So many little girls who are curious about old boxes."

interstage2_4.speaker: speaker1
text: "Be quiet."

interstage2_5.speaker: clara
text: "Why the long face?"
"I'm saying it because I want you to understand."
"Even if you win today..."
"What exactly have you won?"

interstage3_1.speaker: clara
text: "You really think you can defeat me."
"I have been here longer than your church. Longer than your God has had a name."
"I have watched every priest that ever lived return to the dirt."
"And you."
"You are a NOVICE."
"You are afraid. I can feel it. Every prayer you've said since you walked through that door has been half a prayer."

interstage3_2.speaker: clara
text: "So go ahead, finish this."
"SHOW ME WHAT YOU HAVE."

end_start.speaker: narrator
text: There I am.

end_2.speaker: narrator
text: I felt a hand on my shoulder.
We're going to have so much fun.
